segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Today I'm going to talk about the eucalyptus tree.
segments.zh: 今天我要讲的是桉树。在澳大利亚，这种树很常见，有时也被称为胶树。首先我会谈谈它为什么重要，然后描述一下它目前面临的一些问题。

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: This is a very common tree here in Australia, where it's also sometimes called the gum tree.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: First I'm going to talk about why it's important, then I'm going to describe some problems it faces at present.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Right, well the eucalyptus tree is an important tree for lots of reasons.
segments.zh: 好的，桉树之所以重要有很多原因。例如，它为鸟类和蝙蝠等生物提供庇护，这些物种以及其他物种也依赖它获取食物，尤其是它花朵中的花蜜。所以它支持了生物多样性。它对我们人类也有用，因为我们可以用从桉树叶中提取的油制成的消毒剂来杀菌。

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: For example, it gives shelter to creatures like birds and bats, and these and other species also depend on it for food, particularly the nectar from its flowers.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: So it supports biodiversity.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: It's useful to us humans too, because we can kill germs with a disinfectant made from oil extracted from eucalyptus leaves.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: The eucalyptus grows all over Australia and the trees can live for up to four hundred years.
segments.zh: 桉树在澳大利亚各地都有生长，树龄可达四百年。所以令人担忧的是，在全国范围内，桉树的数量正在下降，因为树木正在过早地枯死。那么原因是什么呢？

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: So it's alarming that all across the country, numbers of eucalyptus are falling because the trees are dying off prematurely.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: So what are the reasons for this?

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: One possible reason is disease.
segments.zh: 一个可能的原因是疾病。早在20世纪70年代，树木就开始患上一种叫做“蒙杜拉黄化病”的疾病。树叶会逐渐变黄，然后树木就会死亡。直到2004年，他们才发现问题的原因是石灰，或者用它的化学名称氢氧化钙，它被用于道路建设。石灰被冲刷到地下，影响了附近桉树的根部。它阻止了树木吸收健康生长所需的铁。当铁被重新注入受影响的树木时，它们立即恢复了。

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: As far back as the 1970s the trees started getting a disease called Mundulla Yellows.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: The trees' leaves would gradually turn yellow, then the tree would die.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: It wasn't until 2004 that they found the cause of the problem was lime, or calcium hydroxide to give it its proper chemical name, which was being used in the construction of roads.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: The lime was being washed away into the ground and affecting the roots of the eucalyptus trees nearby.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: What it was doing was preventing the trees from sucking up the iron they needed for healthy growth.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: When this was injected back into the affected trees, they immediately recovered.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: But this problem only affected a relatively small number of trees.
segments.zh: 但这个问题只影响了相对较少的树木。到2000年，澳大利亚东海岸的大量桉树死于一种叫做“铃鸟相关枯梢病”的疾病。铃鸟是一种鸟，这种疾病似乎在铃鸟数量多的地方很常见。同样，受影响的是树叶。昆虫落在树叶上，一路啃食，破坏树叶，同时它们分泌一种含糖的溶液。铃鸟非常喜欢这种溶液，为了尽可能多地获取，它们会赶走其他可能试图获取的动物。所以这些鸟和昆虫以牺牲其他物种为代价而繁盛，最终树叶受到严重损害，树木死亡。

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: By 2000, huge numbers of eucalyptus were dying along Australia's East Coast, of a disease known as Bell-miner Associated Die-back.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: The bell-miner is a bird, and the disease seems to be common where there are high populations of bell-miners.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Again it's the leaves of the trees that are affected.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: What happens is that insects settle on the leaves and eat their way round them, destroying them as they go, and at the same time they secrete a solution which has sugar in it.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: The bell-miner birds really like this solution, and in order to get as much as possible, they keep away other creatures that might try to get it.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: So these birds and insects flourish at the expense of other species, and eventually so much damage is done to the leaves that the tree dies.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: But experts say that trees can start looking sick before any sign of Bell-miner Associated Die-back.
segments.zh: 但专家说，在出现铃鸟相关枯梢病的任何迹象之前，树木可能已经开始看起来不健康了。所以看起来这个问题可能另有原因。一种可能性是与澳大利亚发生的大型丛林火灾有关。生态学家威廉·杰克逊在40多年前提出的一个理论是，特定地区丛林火灾的频率会影响那里生长的植被类型。如果一个地区丛林火灾非常频繁，之后会促进草的生长，而如果丛林火灾不那么频繁，则会导致桉树林的生长。

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: So it looks as if the problem might have another explanation.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: One possibility is that it's to do with the huge bushfires that we have in Australia.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: A theory proposed over 40 years ago by ecologist William Jackson is that the frequency of bushfires in a particular region affects the type of vegetation that grows there.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: If there are very frequent bushfires in a region, this encourages grass to grow afterwards, while if the bushfires are rather less frequent, this results in the growth of eucalyptus forests.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: So why is this?
segments.zh: 那么这是为什么呢？为什么相当频繁的丛林火灾实际上有利于桉树的生长？嗯，一个原因是火灾阻止了其他物种的生长，这些物种会消耗桉树所需的水分。还有另一个原因。如果让这些快速生长的灌木和植物大量繁殖，它们会通过影响土壤成分、去除土壤中的养分来以另一种方式伤害桉树。所以，只要不是太频繁，一些丛林火灾实际上对桉树的生存至关重要。事实上，有证据表明，在欧洲人到来之前，澳大利亚原住民已经进行了数千年的定期焚烧灌木丛。

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Why do fairly frequent bushfires actually support the growth of eucalyptus?

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Well, one reason is that the fire stops the growth of other species which would consume water needed by eucalyptus trees.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: And there's another reason.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: If these other quick-growing species of bushes and plants are allowed to proliferate, they harm the eucalyptus in another way, by affecting the composition of the soil, and removing nutrients from it.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: So some bushfires are actually essential for the eucalyptus to survive as long as they are not too frequent.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: In fact there's evidence that Australia's indigenous people practised regular burning of bush land for thousands of years before the arrival of the Europeans.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: But since Europeans arrived on the continent, the number of bushfires has been strictly controlled.
segments.zh: 但自从欧洲人来到这片大陆以来，丛林火灾的数量一直受到严格控制。现在科学家认为，丛林火灾频率降低到低水平导致了所谓的“干燥雨林”，这听起来很奇怪，因为我们通常将热带雨林与潮湿环境联系在一起。这种雨林有什么特别之处呢？嗯，与生态系统丰富的热带雨林不同，这种生态系统通常很简单。它有非常茂密的植被，但物种多样性不高。植被提供了大量阴凉，所以铃鸟认为这里很理想，它们在灌木丛中筑巢。但这同样对桉树没有帮助。

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Now scientists believe that this reduced frequency of bushfires to low levels has led to what's known as 'dry rainforest', which seems an odd name as usually we associate tropical rainforest with wet conditions.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: And what's special about this type of rainforest?

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Well, unlike tropical rainforest which is a rich ecosystem, this type of ecosystem is usually a simple one.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: It has very thick, dense vegetation, but not much variety of species.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: The vegetation provides lots of shade, so one species that does find it ideal is the bell-miner bird, which builds its nests in the undergrowth there.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: But again that's not helpful for the eucalyptus tree.

items.prompt: The Eucalyptus Tree in Australia: Importance - it provides ... and food for a wide range of species

pairs.note: provides 和 gives 是同义替换，都表示'提供'。
shelter 在题干和原文中完全相同，直接定位。
food 在题干和原文中完全相同，直接定位。

paraphrase.explain: 题干问桉树为多种物种提供什么和食物，原文中'gives shelter to creatures'和'depend on it for food'直接对应，shelter 和 food 都是原词重现。

items.prompt: The Eucalyptus Tree in Australia: Importance - its leaves provide ... which is used to make a disinfectant

pairs.note: 原文直接出现 leaves，与题干相同。
原文直接出现 disinfectant，与题干相同。
原文直接出现 oil，是答案。

paraphrase.explain: 题干问桉树叶提供什么用来做消毒剂，原文说消毒剂由桉树叶提取的油制成，所以答案就是 oil。

items.prompt: The Eucalyptus Tree in Australia: Reasons for present decline in number - 'Mundulla Yellows' - Cause - lime used for making ... was absorbed
The Eucalyptus Tree in Australia: Reasons for present decline in number - 'Bell-miner Associated Die-back' - Cause - ... feed on eucalyptus leaves

pairs.p: insects settle on the leaves and eat their way round them
pairs.note: 题干说'以桉树叶为食'，原文具体描述了昆虫'落在叶子上并啃食'，这是对同一动作的详细解释。

paraphrase.explain: 题干问导致桉树数量下降的原因，定位句提到昆虫在叶子上啃食，所以答案就是'insects'。

items.prompt: The Eucalyptus Tree in Australia: Bushfires - William Jackson's theory: high-frequency bushfires have impact on vegetation, resulting in the growth of ...

pairs.note: high-frequency 替换为 very frequent，意思相同。
答案词 grass 在原文中直接出现。

paraphrase.explain: 题干问高频率山火导致什么植被生长，定位句说非常频繁的山火会促进草类生长，因此答案就是 grass。

items.prompt: The Eucalyptus Tree in Australia: Bushfires - mid-frequency bushfires result in the growth of eucalyptus forests, because they: make more ... available to the trees

pairs.p: consume water needed by eucalyptus trees
pairs.note: 原文说火阻止其他物种消耗水，即火让更多水可用。

paraphrase.explain: 题干问中频山火如何促进桉树生长，定位句提到火阻止其他物种消耗桉树所需的水，因此水变得更多可用。

items.prompt: The Eucalyptus Tree in Australia: Bushfires - mid-frequency bushfires result in the growth of eucalyptus forests, because they: maintain the quality of the ...

pairs.p: affecting the composition of the soil, and removing nutrients from it
pairs.note: 题干说维持质量，原文说影响成分和移除养分，是反义替换，所以答案是soil。

paraphrase.explain: 题干问中频山火如何维持桉树林的生长，定位句提到其他植物会通过影响土壤成分和移除养分来危害桉树，因此山火维持的是土壤质量。

items.prompt: The Eucalyptus Tree in Australia: Bushfires - low-frequency bushfires result in the growth of ... rainforest

pairs.q: low-frequency bushfires result in the growth of ... rainforest
pairs.note: 原文直接出现'dry rainforest'，与题干空格处对应。

paraphrase.explain: 题干问低频山火导致什么雨林生长，原文提到'this reduced frequency of bushfires to low levels has led to what's known as 'dry rainforest''，直接定位到答案'dry'。

items.prompt: The Eucalyptus Tree in Australia: Bushfires - low-frequency bushfires result in the growth of ... rainforest, which is: a ... ecosystem

pairs.p: reduced frequency of bushfires to low levels
pairs.note: 题干用'low-frequency'概括原文'频率降低到低水平'
原文明确是'dry rainforest'，题干省略了'dry'，但定位时需注意

paraphrase.explain: 通过题干关键词'low-frequency bushfires'和'rainforest'定位到原文描述'dry rainforest'的句子，再根据'ecosystem'找到'simple one'对应答案'simple'。

items.prompt: The Eucalyptus Tree in Australia: Bushfires - low-frequency bushfires result in the growth of ... rainforest, which is: an ideal environment for the ... of the bell-miner

pairs.note: 原文直接出现ideal，与题干ideal environment对应。
原文直接出现nests，与答案完全一致。

paraphrase.explain: 定位句提到bell-miner bird builds its nests，直接对应题干中的nest(s)。
